name: Lawrence Brownlee and Friends: The Next Chapter
synopsis: Lyric's Ryan Opera Center presents a free virtual concert celebrating the depth and breadth of Black artistry, hosted by world-renowned tenor Lawrence Brownlee and featuring former and current members of the Ryan Opera Center.

name: For the Love of Lyric Concert
Breaking Down the Score: Attila with Maestro Enrique Mazzola
synopsis: Music Director Designate Enrique Mazzola stars in an original, engaging, and delightful web series centered on the twelve musical numbers of Verdi’s Attila. In each episode, Maestro Mazzola shares a personal interpretation and reading of one of the pieces, offering unique insight into one of Verdi’s early masterpieces from a conductor at the height of his craft.

name: Breaking Down the Score: Attila with Maestro Enrique Mazzola

name: Master Classes with Sir Andrew Davis
synopsis: Lyric takes you behind the scenes as Music Director Sir Andrew Davis leads a series of one-on-one master classes with Ensemble members of the Ryan Opera Center, one of the premier professional artist-development programs in the world. Working with the artists on one of his favorite operas, Mozart's The Marriage of Figaro, Maestro Davis shares detailed insights to help them prepare for international careers.

name: Roll Call: Policing in Chicago's Black Community, Then and Now
synopsis: Join Lyric Opera of Chicago, WFMT, and WTTW for Roll Call: Policing in Chicago's Black Community, Then and Now.  This virtual conversation will focus on the evolving relationship between the Chicago Police Department (CPD) and Chicago's Black community.

name: Breaking Down the Score: Attila with Maestro Enrique Mazzola
synopsis: Music Director Designate Enrique Mazzola stars in an original, engaging, and delightful web series centered on the twelve musical numbers of Verdi’s Attila. In each episode, Maestro Mazzola shares a personal interpretation and reading of one of the pieces, offering unique insight into one of Verdi’s early masterpieces from a conductor at the height of his craft.

name: Pasión Latina
synopsis: Celebrate music of the Spanish-speaking world with a free virtual concert starring and hosted by renowned soprano and Lyric favorite Ana María Martínez.

name: Breaking Down the Score: Attila with Maestro Enrique Mazzola
synopsis: Music Director Designate Enrique Mazzola stars in an original, engaging, and delightful web series centered on the twelve musical numbers of Verdi’s Attila. In each episode, Maestro Mazzola shares a personal interpretation and reading of one of the pieces, offering unique insight into one of Verdi’s early masterpieces from a conductor at the height of his craft.

name: Sole e Amore (Sun and Love)
synopsis: Revel in the sounds of Italy with a virtual recital awash with beautiful lesser-known musical gems by beloved Italian opera composers, curated and hosted by Lyric’s Music Director Designate Enrique Mazzola and presented by The Patrick G. and Shirley W. Ryan Opera Center. With Maestro Mazzola at the piano, members of the Ryan Opera Center 2020/21 Ensemble will perform songs by Rossini, Donizetti, Bellini, Verdi, Puccini, and Mascagni, selected with love by Mazzola.

name: Attila Highlights in Concert: Explore More with Enrique Mazzola
synopsis: Music Director Designate Enrique Mazzola leads world-renown principal artists in a performance of piano-accompanied excerpts from Verdi’s Attila, the story of the infamous “Scourge of God,” his consuming desire for power, and his final downfall.

name: Celebrating Sir Andrew Davis, from Mozart to Stravinsky
synopsis: Join us to raise a glass to Sir Andrew — and to more than three decades of his glorious music-making!

name: The New Classics: Songs from the New Golden Age of Musical Theater
Sunday in the Park with Lyric's Rising Stars
location: Jay Pritzker Pavilion, Millennium Park, 201 E Randolph St

name: There's No Place Like Lyric: Season Opening Gala
2022 Halloween Masquerade
location: Lyric Opera of Chicago (20 N Wacker Drive, Chicago, IL 60606)
synopsis: The Lyric Young Professionals cordially invite you to a Halloween dinner, and murder is on the menu…

name: A Salute to the Seventies
location: The Casino Club of Chicago: 195 East Delaware Place
synopsis: The Patrick G. and Shirley W. Ryan Opera Center’s 8th annual gala, A Salute to the Seventies, will be held on Monday, February 6, at The Casino Club of Chicago. A highlight of the evening will be an amazing program performed by Ryan Opera Center Ensemble Members and Music Director Craig Terry.

name: 2024 Ryan Opera Center Gala
synopsis: The Patrick G. and Shirley W. Ryan Opera Center’s 9th annual gala, Carnivale!, will be held on Monday, February 12, 2024, at The Casino Club. It will feature a fabulously entertaining performance of operatic and popular music by Ryan Opera Center Ensemble Members and Music Director Craig Terry.

name: Art-See Jamboree
location: East Lawn, Millennium Park, 201 E Randolph St, Chicago.

name: Lyric Opening Gala 2024
synopsis: Join us on Friday, October 4th to celebrate the opening of Lyric’s season with the Lyric Opening Gala and a tremendous concert by star of stage and screen, Patti LuPone. Walk the red carpet and enjoy pre-performance cocktails and hors d'oeuvres, dinner following the concert, then dance the night away!

name: The Patrick G. and Shirley W. Ryan Opera Center 50th Anniversary Golden Celebrations
Lyric Opera of Chicago Great Choral Works
Lyric Opening Gala
location: Lyric Opera of Chicago, 20 N. Wacker Drive
synopsis: Join us on Friday, October 10, for Lyric Lights Up Broadway: A Night of Glamour and Music — a thrilling night of entertainment featuring Tony Award winners Brian Stokes Mitchell and Laura Benanti in concert.

name: Moonlight Social
synopsis: Love is in the air, the music is soaring and the Moonlight Social returns — more dazzling than ever! Join the Lyric Young Professionals for the second annual Moonlight Social, celebrating one of opera’s most beloved productions, Così fan tutte!

name: Ryan Opera Center Gala 2026
location: The Casino Club of Chicago, 195 East Delaware Place
synopsis: The Patrick G. and Shirley W. Ryan Opera Center’s 11th annual gala, Fête du Soleil, will be held on Tuesday, February 17, 2026, at The Casino Club. It will feature an amazing performance of operatic and popular music by Ryan Opera Center Ensemble artists and Music Director Craig Terry.

name: Civic Night at Lyric: Madama Butterfly
Sunday in the Park with Lyric
location: Jay Pritzker Pavilion, Millennium Park, 201 E Randolph St, Chicago.
synopsis: Bring a blanket, your favorite snacks, your fellow music lovers, and those you can't wait to introduce to opera for this free, fun and friendly, can't-miss summer delight that always ushers in a new cultural season on a high note.

accessibilityInfo.title: SoundShirt
accessibilityInfo.name: SoundShirt
accessibilityInfo.modalDescription: The SoundShirt uses touch sensation to allow wearers the ability to feel music on their upper body, in real time with the orchestra and singers. SoundShirts must be reserved online or by calling the Audiences Services team at 312.827.5600.

accessibilityInfo.title: Audio-described performance
accessibilityInfo.name: Audio-described performance
accessibilityInfo.modalDescription: Audio-described performances include a narration which guides the listener through the presentation with concise, objective descriptions of new scenes, settings, costumes, and body language, communicated to the patron through headphones at appropriate times throughout the performance. Devices may be borrowed from coat check 1 on the main floor.

accessibilityInfo.title: ASL-interpreted performance
accessibilityInfo.name: ASL-interpreted performance
accessibilityInfo.modalDescription: All dialogue and lyrics will be translated into American Sign Language by two certified interpreters located near the stage. Select seats are reserved to give you the best sight lines to follow the interpretation and all the action on stage.
